fix(ci): restore release workflow task names

Summary:
- update the release workflow to call the renamed Gradle tasks
  for install verification and distribution archives
- switch release artifact paths, formula output, and artifact naming
  from `ccode` to `agent`

Rationale:
- release tagging was failing immediately because the workflow still
  invoked removed `ccode` task names
- keeping the workflow aligned with the renamed distribution artifacts
  avoids follow-on failures later in the release job

Tests:
- `./gradlew test verifyAgentInstallDist agentDistTar agentDistZip`

commit.message:
fix(git): ignore .agent workspace state

Summary:
- add a repo-wide ignore rule for `.agent/`
- keep the legacy `.crackedcode-agent/` ignore rule in place

Rationale:
- the default workspace state directory now lives under `.agent/`
- ignoring it prevents session state and config files from showing up
  as untracked files or being committed by accident

Tests:
- git check-ignore -v .agent .agent/config.properties

commit.message:
refactor(userscript): 将倍速切换改为全局持久化 (#15)

## Why
- 现有脚本把直播页当作特殊分支处理，抽象上不统一，也不符合当前希望的“全局速度状态”模型。
- 需要让快捷键切换结果跨页面保留，避免每个页面重新进入后恢复默认值。

## What
- 删除直播页检测与强制锁定 `1x` 的逻辑，所有页面统一按同一个速度状态处理。
- 使用 `GM_getValue` / `GM_setValue` 持久化当前速度，并在页面初始化与站内跳转后重新读取应用。
- 更新 userscript 元数据说明、授权项与版本号。

## Testing
- 运行 `node --check scripts/MediaSpeedToggle.user.js`
- 未补自动化测试：仓库当前没有针对该 userscript 的现成测试框架

## Risks
- Breaking change：直播页面不再强制保持
`1x`，原先依赖该行为的使用方式会失效；如需恢复，只能在后续重新引入站点或场景级规则。

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ci: upgrade base images lost in PR #62 merge conflict (issue #65) (#66)

PR #62 (supply chain hardening) was based on the pre-dependabot state
and overwrote two base image upgrades that had already landed on main:
- PR #60: openresty 1.25.3.2-jammy → 1.29.2.1-jammy
- PR #59: ubuntu 22.04 → 24.04

Restores both upgrades with pinned digests:
- openresty 1.29.2.1-jammy@sha256:01ae8007b5a26967ad8158554591c889345be3ffe51424fe475beb38ed923692
- ubuntu 24.04@sha256:67efaecc0031a612cf7bb3c863407018dbbef0a971f62032b77aa542ac8ac0d2

Updates supply_chain_pins_spec.lua to match the new openresty digest.

Closes #65.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(preview): stabilize rounded camera preview edges

Summary:
- clip the preview container layer with the same continuous corner radius
- remove the extra display-layer background fill behind the sample buffer
- expand display-layer geometry to backing-pixel boundaries on layout and
  backing-scale changes

Rationale:
- the preview could land on fractional point sizes under the SwiftUI 16:9
  layout, which left the sample buffer display layer on a half-pixel edge
- snapping the display layer to backing pixels and clipping it inside the
  AppKit layer avoids the bottom-edge seam against the rounded mask

Tests:
- xcodebuild -project AutoFrameCam.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
- xcodebuild -project AutoFrameCam.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO test
- runtime app launch succeeded, but screenshot/accessibility evidence was
  blocked by local macOS privacy permissions

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct OPM grep pattern in nightly security check (#64)

The lua-resty-maxminddb version check was using a space separator
pattern ('lua-resty-maxminddb \K[0-9.]+') that no longer matches
the Dockerfile after issue #56 changed the opm get syntax to use
an equals sign ('opm get package=version').

Empty PINNED caused the step to always enter the "update available"
branch and fail, which in turn triggered the "Open issue on nightly
security failure" step — creating issue #63.

Fixes #63.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: harden pinned supply chain dependencies (issue #56) (#62)

* Harden pinned supply chain dependencies

* Add regression tests for dependency pinning

* Pin test Docker base image by digest

* fix(docker): correct opm version syntax — use = not space (issue #56)

OPM version specification uses `package=version` syntax, not space-
separated. `opm get anjia0532/lua-resty-maxminddb 1.3.7` treated 1.3.7
as a separate dependency name, causing exit code 25.

regression test pattern to match the correct form:

feat(predict): add shape parameter for non-square inference (#866)

* feat(predict): add shape parameter for non-square inference (#682)

RFDETR.predict() previously ignored a shape kwarg and always resized
inputs to the square (resolution, resolution) — making it impossible to
run inference matching a non-square ONNX export. Add an explicit
shape: Optional[tuple[int, int]] parameter that, when provided, overrides
the default square resize, consistent with the existing export(shape=…) API.

Also fix the _optimized_resolution mismatch guard to compare both height
and width (previously only height was checked), and validate that any
provided shape has both dimensions divisible by 14 (same constraint as
export()).

- Add Raises: section to predict() docstring covering all four
  ValueError paths introduced by the shape parameter
- Document the divisible-by-14 constraint in the shape param description
- Reject bool values in shape validation (bool is a subclass of int
  but semantically invalid as a dimension)
- Add blank line between shape validation block and the inference
  optimisation warning for readability
- Remove stale # type: ignore[misc] — Optional[tuple[int, int]] is
  sufficiently typed after the `if shape is not None:` guard
- Add 8 parametrized test cases covering float dims, wrong arity
  (1- and 3-element), zero/negative, and bool dimensions
- Add CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] ### Added (closes #682)
- Add match="shape" to test_predict_shape_invalid_raises so any
  ValueError unrelated to the shape parameter cannot silently pass
- Add negative_width case (-14 width) to complete the negative-dim
  symmetry with existing negative_height
- Parametrize test_predict_shape_not_divisible_by_14_raises to cover
  height-not-divisible path alongside the existing width path
- Apply ruff type-annotation modernisation (Optional[...] → ... | None,
  bare tuple → tuple[int | float | bool, ...])
- Add support for integer-like types (e.g., numpy, torch) via the `__index__` protocol.
- Improve shape validation error messages and clarify bool rejection.
- Update test coverage to ensure acceptance of integer-like dimensions.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(memory): add typed memory schema foundation

Summary:
- add structured memory tables, repository helpers, and foreign-key enforcement for typed memories, entities, sources, edges, and snapshots
- dual-write session consolidation into the structured store while preserving the existing vector path and audit partial success when the writes diverge
- cover the substrate with migration, repository, and consolidation tests plus a Batch A review log entry

Rationale:
- make Batch A start from a real durable memory substrate instead of an inert schema sketch
- keep the current memory path working while exposing enough structure for later kind, linking, and snapshot slices
- catch migration drift and split-write inconsistencies before richer retrieval depends on this layer

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/db/engine.py backend/src/db/models.py backend/src/memory/repository.py backend/src/memory/consolidator.py backend/tests/conftest.py backend/tests/test_db_engine.py backend/tests/test_memory_repository.py backend/tests/test_consolidator.py backend/tests/test_consolidation_reliability.py
- backend/.venv/bin/python -m pytest backend/tests/test_consolidator.py -q
- backend/.venv/bin/python -m pytest backend/tests/test_db_engine.py backend/tests/test_memory_repository.py backend/tests/test_consolidation_reliability.py backend/tests/test_vector_store.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py -q

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): tighten overview market status refresh

Summary:
- guard decision-history fetches so stale responses cannot overwrite the
  current overview market focus
- remove the unused dashboard performance fetch and limit status markets to
  today's activity or current open positions
- document the narrowed status-market contract and add regression tests for
  stale market filtering and fetch ordering

Rationale:
- review follow-up identified a real UI race when delayed history responses
  arrived after the operator changed markets
- overview status should stay focused on active markets instead of surfacing
  historical-only markets with no current position exposure

Tests:
- pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "stale_markets_without_today_activity_or_positions or stale_decision_fetch_from_overwriting_market_focus" -v
- pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -v
- ruff check src/ tests/
- python3 scripts/validate_docs_sync.py
- pytest -v --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(storefront): return 404 for unmatched paths and normalize blog redirect (#875)

* fix(storefront): return 404 for unmatched paths and normalize blog redirect

Invalid storefront paths (e.g., /product/some-slug, /product-tag/samsung)
were falling through to the homepage and returning 200 instead of 404.
This caused soft-404 SEO issues. Fix reads x-pathname header to detect
when no child route matched and calls notFound().

Also normalizes malformed blog.ogabassey.com paths that contain an
accidental domain prefix (e.g., /ogabassey.com/blog/...) before
redirecting, preventing double-domain URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing headers import for storefront 404 check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: trigger CI and CodeRabbit review

* fix(proxy): handle blog redirect edge cases

- Bare domain path (/ogabassey.com) now redirects to /blog/ instead of
  producing a broken URL
- Paths already containing /blog/ are stripped to prevent double /blog/blog/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(proxy): use explicit domain allowlist instead of regex

Replace overmatch-prone dotted-segment regex with an explicit Set of
known accidental domain prefixes. Prevents /favicon.ico or /sitemap.xml
from being misidentified as domain prefixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(proxy): derive root domain dynamically instead of hardcoding

Strip the blog. prefix from the hostname to get the root domain,
so the accidental-prefix detection adapts automatically without
maintaining a hardcoded allowlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): handle path-mode homepage in 404 check

In dev path-mode, x-pathname is /{slug} for the homepage (not /).
Allow both / and /{slug} as valid homepage pathnames to prevent
a false 404 in development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability on cron secret endpoints (#877)

* 🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability in cron APIs

Replaced strict string inequality checks (`!==`) on cron secrets with constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual` to prevent timing attacks across multiple `apps/web/src/app/api/cron/*` endpoints. Added preliminary length checks before comparison to avoid unhandled buffer length exceptions in Node's crypto library.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cron): align cleanup-orders auth with x-cron-secret pattern and add missing blank line

- Switch cleanup-orders from Authorization Bearer header to x-cron-secret
  header to match all other cron endpoints
- Add blank line before heading in sentinel.md for proper markdown formatting
- Add cleanup-orders to vercel.json crons array (daily at 2 AM)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* 🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability in cron APIs

Replaced strict string inequality checks (`!==`) on cron secrets with constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual` to prevent timing attacks across multiple `apps/web/src/app/api/cron/*` endpoints. Added preliminary length checks before comparison to avoid unhandled buffer length exceptions in Node's crypto library.

commit.message:
Work (#7)

* Revamp dashboard, clients and appointments UI; add Conversations & Automations pages; reorganize settings/system/sidebar

### Motivation
- Simplify and refocus several admin screens to surface operational actions and reduce clutter from deep analytics and rarely-used filters.
- Provide compact, action-oriented summaries and visualizations so teams can quickly spot issues (AI handoffs, cancellations, errors) and act.
- Introduce two new working screens for monitoring dialog flow (`conversations`) and automation scenarios (`automations`).
- Reorganize navigation and settings to separate core MVP actions from legacy/secondary functionality.

### Description
- Reworked the dashboard (`app/page.tsx`) to show AI-centric KPIs and small charts rendered with `recharts`, added automation summary cards, and simplified recent-activity rendering; replaced several legacy charts/components with compact area/bar charts and attention cards.
- Rebuilt the appointments view (`app/appointments/page.tsx`) to surface operational status cards, a status bar chart, and an inline simplified table instead of the previous full-featured table and funnel/heatmap visualization.
- Simplified the clients screen (`app/clients/page.tsx`) by removing a large set of advanced filters and campaign UI, replacing them with lightweight actionable filters, summary metric cards, and a concise client table with scenario tags and next-appointment info.
- Added `app/conversations/page.tsx` to monitor inbound dialog flow, channel distribution and AI vs human handling with chart and list views.
- Added `app/automations/page.tsx` to present scenario metrics, health indicators and simple controls for automation scenarios.
- Updated system and settings pages (`app/system/page.tsx`, `app/settings/page.tsx`) to reorganize sections, add anchors/IDs for intra-page navigation, and surface core controls (agent toggle, knowledge uploads, roles, notifications) with clearer labels.
- Updated the sidebar (`components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`) to reflect new navigation structure, introduce a small "Future / Legacy" section for less-used links, and tune item rendering behavior for anchor/hash links.
- Misc: removed several complex helper utilities and UI modules from these pages, introduced `formatDate`/`formatCurrency` usages, adjusted icons and color palettes, and added small UX text/content changes across pages.

### Testing
- Performed a local type-check with `tsc --noEmit`, which completed successfully.
- Built the application with `yarn build` and verified the production build step completed without errors.
- Ran linting (`yarn lint`) and unit test command (`yarn test`) where available; these commands completed without reported failures.

* Restore data-driven admin screens and navigation

* Adjust settings page layout

* Move auto-systems section to settings

* Add period selector to dashboard revenue chart

* Refine revenue chart range and period pickers

* Switch revenue date picker to date-based options

* Limit revenue date options by registration and show data guard

* Rename revenue block title on dashboard

* Fix dashboard revenue source and resilient appointments load

* Make dashboard metric cards more compact

* Increase compact KPI value size and offsets

* Reduce compact KPI card footprint

* Adjust KPI title/description colors and badge size

* Use Unbounded font for metric card titles

* Replace analytics monthly revenue chart with period money movement chart

* Show revenue chart when at least one point is available

* Match Analytics KPI card design to Dashboard metric cards

* Move analytics communication volume banner to page header section

* Place communication volume banner to the right of period selector

* Rename analytics trends title to use slash separator

* Match communication banner backing style to period selector

* Reduce trends header backing height to match period block

* Make analytics top control blocks use equal backing height

* Add full analytics period selector with contextual date options

* Restrict analytics date options to periods intersecting registration date

* Hide past dates from analytics period date selector

* Show registration-start label in date block for all-time period

* Use real org registration date source for analytics period labels

* Reduce compact metric value offsets from right and bottom

* Prevent services card from stretching to chart row height

* Raise recent activity block and lower appointments block for aligned bottoms

* Restructure homepage columns to move recent activity and appointments up

* Restore revenue block height and move appointments row upward

* Lift recent activity card upward toward services block

* Fix dashboard card overlap in right column

* Stack dashboard right column blocks with consistent spacing

* Prevent revenue chart card from stretching vertically

* Extend daily appointments block to align with activity block

* Update page.tsx

* Scale down main interface density on desktop

* Revert desktop scale adjustment in main layout

* Move autosystems block from settings to system page

* Translate remaining English UI labels to Russian

* Update pricing plans with new names descriptions and pricing

* Make pricing cards full-width on system page

* Lay out pricing cards in one full-width row

* Lock Growth plan selection and swap scaling CTA

* Rename Growth plan and update soon badge

* Update page.tsx

* Remove prices from pricing cards

feat(dashboard): refine market status summary

Summary:
- enrich `/api/status` with market operating summary fields for positions,
  latest decision context, market-level circuit breaker state, and
  derived status tone
- split the dashboard into `Overview` and `Diagnostics` surfaces and
  link only the overview `market` focus across summary, chart, and
  decision history
- document the dashboard linkage contract and add/update design,
  implementation, and dashboard regression tests

Rationale:
- operators need market-scoped status at the top of the dashboard,
  while deeper diagnostics should not compete with the main operating
  view
- restricting shared state to the `market` dimension keeps drill-down
  behavior explicit without coupling every history filter to summary
  and chart panels

Tests:
- python3 scripts/session_handover_check.py --strict
- pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "market_operating_summary or overview_and_diagnostics_surfaces or overview_market_linkage_rules" -v
- pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -v
- ruff check src/ tests/
- python3 scripts/validate_docs_sync.py
- pytest -v --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing

commit.message:
feat(symphony): add GitHub Projects v2 tracker support (#10)

Summary:
- add a GitHub Projects v2 tracker client with startup validation,
  project-item paging, blocker state resolution, and cached item/state
  metadata for tracker refreshes
- make Codex dynamic GraphQL tools tracker-aware so Linear keeps
  `linear_graphql` and GitHub workflows expose `github_graphql`
- update workflow examples, docs, and dashboard wording for
  tracker-neutral prompts and dual tracker support

Rationale:
- Symphony was hard-wired to Linear in tracker construction,
  parameter helpers, and dynamic tool registration, which blocked
  GitHub Projects v2 workflows end to end
- GitHub issue state has to come from the configured Project v2 field
  rather than open/closed state so Todo blocker gating and runtime
  refreshes stay consistent with workflow state names

Tests:
- /opt/homebrew/bin/go test ./pkg/symphony/config ./pkg/symphony/tracker ./pkg/symphony/tracker/github ./pkg/symphony/provider ./pkg/symphony/codex ./pkg/symphony/orchestrator
- /opt/homebrew/bin/go test ./pkg/symphony/...
- /opt/homebrew/bin/go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add post-resolve validate and release v1.5.0

Validate
- Implement `validate` as a Proc-based runtime option.
- Run `validate` after all parameters finish resolving, coercing, transforming, and writing into `px`.
- Execute `validate` in the current controller context so callbacks can read `px` and call controller methods such as `current_user`.
- Short-circuit the post-validation pass when an endpoint tree contains no `validate` callbacks.

Schema DSL
- Make `type: { ... }` build nested object schemas.
- Support object-level defaults for `type: { ... }`.
- Make `type: []` equivalent to bare `[]`.
- Keep `type: Hash` equivalent to `type: Object`.

Docs And Tests
- Sync the English and Chinese README for nested object timing, whitelist extraction, `type` keyword semantics, and the new `validate` option.
- Add regression coverage for nested object execution order, custom validation timing, `type: { ... }`, `type: []`, and object defaults.
- Align CI and gem metadata with the Ruby 3.1 support baseline.

Verification
- Run `bundle exec rspec`.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(feed): use canonical category-based product URLs in feeds (#876)

Google Merchant and OpenAI feeds were generating incorrect product URLs.
GMC hardcoded /products/{slug} which 301-redirects (penalized by Google).
OpenAI feed incorrectly included merchant slug in URL path and used raw
category text instead of proper slugs.

Both feeds now use buildProductUrl() from seo-utils.ts which generates
the canonical /{category-slug}/{product-slug} pattern. The OpenAI feed
also resolves custom domains via buildMerchantBaseUrl() instead of
deriving URLs from the request host header.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Work (#110)

* Make weekly leaderboard a live hosted weekly-best competition

### Motivation
- Convert the existing weekly leaderboard from a multi-run local-fallback model into a hosted-only live weekly competition that ranks players by their best single score for the current UTC week.
- Prevent local fallbacks from showing stale or offline data in the hosted leaderboard card and ensure consistent server-side validation and tie-break rules.

### Description
- Database: removed use of `counted_runs`, added a reinforced index and an RPC `upsert_weekly_best_leaderboard_entry(...)` which inserts-or-updates with `total_score = greatest(existing_best, submitted_score)` and uses `created_at ASC` as a stable tie-break for equal scores; added migration `supabase/migrations/202603251100_weekly_best_live_competition.sql` and updated `supabase/weekly_leaderboard.sql` accordingly.
- Edge Function: updated `supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts` to validate a single non-negative integer `total_score`, keep trusted `player_id` from the JWT and server-side handle lookup, and call the new RPC so lower scores do not overwrite a higher weekly best; returned value reports the stored weekly best.
- Frontend: removed the local leaderboard fallback for the hosted card, hid the leaderboard card when offline, when Supabase config is missing, or when the hosted fetch fails, and added lightweight polling for live refresh on the weekly page with proper cleanup when leaving the page; updated UI copy to reflect the weekly-best competition and show rank, handle, best score and league lines.
- Docs and agent snapshot: updated `README.md` and `AGENTS.md` to document the weekly competition rules, the best-single-score ranking, and the hosted-only leaderboard card behaviour.
- Files changed include `supabase/weekly_leaderboard.sql`, `supabase/migrations/202603251100_weekly_best_live_competition.sql`, `supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts`, `app.js`, `index.html`, `README.md`, and `AGENTS.md`.
- Migration notes: run `supabase db push` or apply the new migration file; the migration drops `counted_runs` and creates the RPC used by the Edge Function; re-deploy the `upsert-leaderboard-entry` Edge Function after the migration so it uses the new RPC.

### Testing
- Static site validation: ran `bash ./scripts/validate-static-site.sh` which passed.
- JavaScript sanity check: ran `node --check app.js` which completed without syntax errors.
- Edge Function type check: attempted `deno check supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts` but `deno` is not available in this environment, so server-side type checking should be run in CI or locally with Deno; function logic was updated to call the new RPC and remaining behaviour is covered by the migration.
- Note: runtime verification against a Supabase instance (apply migration, deploy Edge Function, exercise writes and live reads) is recommended in staging to confirm RLS, service role grant and live polling behaviour.

* Simplify weekly page to hosted global leaderboard only

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add scope compaction and schemas bang control

Scope
- Add `compact` and `compact_blank` options to `scope`.
- Apply these options to the custom `px.scope[:name]` bucket after validation.
- Keep the behavior shallow and leave top-level `px` plus built-in scope buckets unchanged.

Schemas
- Add `bang:` to `.schemas` with a default of `true`.
- Keep bang keys such as `name!` by default.
- When `bang: false`, emit plain keys and write requiredness as `required: true`.
- Keep `only:` compatible with bang-style names even when bang output is disabled.

Docs And Tests
- Sync the English and Chinese README examples and explanations.
- Add regression coverage for scope options and `.schemas(bang: false)`.

Verification
- Run `bundle exec rspec`.
